content.thinking:
Good. I can see:
1. A 3-node CockroachDB cluster running in the `cockroachdb` namespace
2. Services: `crdb-cluster` (headless) and `crdb-cluster-public` (ClusterIP) on ports 26257/TCP and 8080/TCP
3. ingress-nginx controller running, but no `--tcp-services-configmap` arg yet
4. A TLS secret `crdb-ui-tls` already exists in the cockroachdb namespace
5. ingress-nginx controller service on ports 80 and 443 (no 26257 yet)
Now I need to handle two things: setting up HTTPS for the DB Console at `crdb-ui.example.com` by deciding whether nginx should terminate TLS using the existing cert or pass through to the backend's own TLS, and exposing the TCP port 26257 through ingress-nginx using a TCP services configmap. The `crdb-ui-tls` secret is likely for nginx TLS termination, so I'll configure nginx to terminate TLS and proxy to CockroachDB's HTTPS backend using the `HTTPS` backend protocol annotation. For the SQL port, I need to set up the `tcp-services` configmap in the ingress-nginx namespace to route port 26257 to the CockroachDB service, add the configmap reference to the controller deployment args, and expose that port on the ingress controller's Service.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o yaml 2>&1 | grep -A 5 'http\|8080\|insecure\|tls' | head -40
input.description: Check CockroachDB statefulset for TLS/insecure config

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-ui-tls -o yaml 2>&1 | head -20

content.thinking:
CockroachDB is running in `--insecure` mode, so port 8080 serves plain HTTP. The `crdb-ui-tls` secret has dummy TLS cert/key (base64 of "dummy-crt" and "dummy-key"). So:

1. For the DB Console Ingress: nginx terminates TLS using the `crdb-ui-tls` secret and proxies HTTP to backend port 8080. Backend protocol is HTTP (insecure mode).
2. For SQL TCP: configure TCP services configmap for port 26257.
3. I need to create an Ingress resource pointing to `crdb-ui.example.com` with TLS termination via the `crdb-ui-tls` secret, routing to the `crdb-cluster-public` service on port 8080 using the nginx ingress class.
4. I also need to update the `tcp-services` configmap in the `ingress-nginx` namespace to expose port 26257 for SQL connections to the same service.
5. Then I'll patch the ingress-nginx controller deployment to reference this configmap and add port 26257 to the ingress-nginx-controller Service itself.

Writing the manifests...

content.text:
CockroachDB is running in `--insecure` mode (plain HTTP on 8080), so nginx will terminate TLS and proxy HTTP to the backend. The `crdb-ui-tls` secret already exists for that purpose.

Plan:
1. Create the `tcp-services` configmap in `ingress-nginx` with port 26257 mapped to CockroachDB SQL
2. Patch the ingress-nginx controller deployment to add `--tcp-services-configmap`
3. Patch the ingress-nginx-controller Service to expose port 26257
4. Create an Ingress for the DB Console with TLS

Let me apply all of this.

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: tcp-services
  namespace: ingress-nginx
data:
  "26257": "cockroachdb/crdb-cluster-public:26257"
EOF
input.description: Create tcp-services ConfigMap for CockroachDB SQL port
Patch ingress-nginx controller to use tcp-services configmap
Expose TCP port 26257 on the ingress-nginx-controller Service
